summary: This page summarizes key special values of complex exponentials on the unit circle, including cases for angles that are integer multiples of pi and pi/2, and presents them in Table B.1. It also explains how the magnitude-angle interpretation of re^{j\theta} helps analyze the behavior of e^{(\alpha+j\omega)t} as t approaches infinity, showing that the real exponential term determines growth or decay. The page concludes with a warning about using inverse tangent on calculators to compute complex angles, emphasizing correct quadrant identification.
